qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded Kademlia peer address parsing enables memory exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded Kademlia peer/address collection in protocols/kad/src/protocol.rs; the advisory concerns libp2p-gossipsub PRUNE.backoff Duration/Instant overflow during Gossipsub backoff handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Gossipsub PRUNE.backoff overflow root cause or the associated time arithmetic panic path. The only final qualifying finding targets Kademlia response parsing and memory exhaustion.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gc42-3jg7-rxr2 describes libp2p-gossipsub accepting attacker-controlled PRUNE backoff values that can overflow Duration/Instant arithmetic and panic.
Judge input manifest lists one final qualifying finding: kademlia_multiaddr_dos.

kademlia_multiaddr_dos reports unbounded processing of Kademlia closerPeers/providerPeers and peer.addrs in protocols/kad/src/protocol.rs, which is a different protocol, source path, and root-cause mechanism.
